q: Khin Khin is more intelligent than Tin Tin.
a: Tin Tin is not as intelligent as Khin Khin.

q: An aeroplane travels much faster than a train.
a: A train doesn't travel as fast as an aeroplane.

q: My father is taller than my uncle.
a: My uncle is not as tall as my father.

q: Gold is not as expensive as platinum.
a: Platinum is more expensive than gold.

q: Reading is easier than writing.
a: Writing is not as easy as reading.

q: Thura can run much faster than Zaw Zaw.
a: Zaw Zaw can't run as fast as Thura.

q: Learning to swim is easier than learning computer science.
a: Learning computer science is not as easy as learning to swim.

q: A whale is bigger than an elephant.
a: An elephant is not as big as a whale.

q: Classical music is more pleasant than pop music.
a: Pop music is not as pleasant as classical music.

q: Htoo Thar is not as hardworking as Ngwe Soe.
a: Ngwe Soe is more hardworking than Htoo Thar.

q: Chan Tha dances more skilfully than Moe Win.
a: Moe Win is not as skilful as Chan Tha.

q: Kay Thwe is more intelligent than Nay Nwe.
a: Nay Nwe is not as intelligent as Kay Thwe.

q: Grandfather clocks required more space than other styles of clocks.
a: Other styles of clocks do not require as much space as grandfather clocks.

q: A Toyota saloon is not as expensive as a Mercedes car.
a: A Mercedes car is more expensive than a Toyota saloon.

q: Boys are more playful than girls.
a: Girls are not as playful as boys.

q: The speaking skill is more difficult than the reading skill.
a: The reading skill is not as difficult as the speaking skill.

q: My sister arrived home earlier than I did.
a: I did not arrive home as early as my sister.

q: Chemistry is more difficult to learn than physics.
a: Physics is not as difficult to learn as Chemistry.

q: My mother gets up earlier than I do.
a: I do not get up as early as my mother.

q: It is colder in January than in December.
a: December is not as cold as January.

q: Myo Myo ran much faster than Swe Swe in the last race.
a: Swe Swe did not run as fast as Myo Myo in the last race.

q: A diamond is more expensive than a ruby.
a: A ruby is not as expensive as a diamond.

q: My sister can play the piano better than I do.
a: I cannot play the piano as well as my sister does.

q: My mother drives more carefully than my father.
a: My father does not drive as carefully as my mother.

q: Mg Mg doesn't speak English as fluently as Ma Ma.
a: Ma Ma speaks English more fluently than Mg Mg.

q: Nobody loves me as much as my parents.
a: My parents love me more than anybody.

q: Nobody trusts me as much as my teacher.
a: My teacher trusts me more than anybody.

q: Nobody is kinder to me than my grandma.
a: My grandma is kinder to me than anybody.

q: Nobody knows this subject as much as U Pwint Oo.
a: U Pwint Oo knows this subject more than anybody.

q: Nobody plays football as beautiful as Pele.
a: Pele plays football more beautifully than anybody.

q: This book is more interesting than that one.
a: That book is not as interesting as this one.

q: Life in the countryside is more peaceful than in the city.
a: Life in the city is not as peaceful as in the countryside.

q: Maria works more carefully than John.
a: John does not work as carefully as Maria.

q: A dolphin is more intelligent than a dog.
a: A dog is not as intelligent as a dolphin.

q: The new hotel is more luxurious than the old one.
a: The old hotel is not as luxurious as the new one.

q: Tom is more creative than his classmates.
a: His classmates are not as creative as Tom.

q: Our school trip was more exciting than we expected.
a: We expected the trip to be not as exciting as it actually was.

q: A train journey is more relaxing than a car ride.
a: A car ride is not as relaxing as a train journey.

q: My presentation was more detailed than yours.
a: Your presentation was not as detailed as mine.

q: She speaks French more fluently than her brother.
a: Her brother does not speak French as fluently as she does.

q: The second film was more boring than the first one.
a: The first film was not as boring as the second one.

q: Jane is more confident than she used to be.
a: She used to be not as confident as she is now.

q: This mobile app is more useful than the older version.
a: The older version is not as useful as this mobile app.

q: His solution is more practical than mine.
a: My solution is not as practical as his.

q: The teacher's explanation was clearer than the textbook.
a: The textbook's explanation was not as clear as the teacher's.
